$comment: c80 (D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-SYMBOL-BASE-INDEX, top-level scalar form; 2026-07-02): a file-scope pointer global initialized to `&arr[CONST-EXPR]` is an ADDRESS CONSTANT (C 6.6p9) -> folds to an abs64 reloc with a stride-scaled addend (`.quad arr+K*sizeof(elem)`, = gcc), never a runtime store. The exact sqlite construct: sqlite3aLTb/aEQb/aGTb = &sqlite3UpperToLower[256-OP_Ne] (3 of the 9 K_NoMatchingObjectFormat runtime-init globals, the last compile-stage sqlite frontier). Pre-fix tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr's AddressOf arm accepted only AddressOf(Ref) (addend 0) -> the init fell to runtimeInit -> K_NoMatchingObjectFormat fail-loud. FIX: tryResolveConstLvaluePath (Ref -> {sym,0}; Index -> +idx*elementStride; MemberAccess -> +fieldByteOffset; conservative gates: Index base must be Array-typed, MemberAccess base Struct/Union). Checks are BY VALUE through the pointers (incl. a stride>1 int table) — deliberately no `p == arr` identity check (the unrelated D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-POINTER-IDENTITY deferral). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the resolver -> K_NoMatchingObjectFormat. => 42.
